data.narrative:
New Limit is a Series C biotech and health company founded by Brian Armstrong, Blake Byers, and Jacob Kimmel, currently valued at $3.1B post-money following $610M+ in cumulative fundraising. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 3 public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private companies at similar or adjacent stages, including Colossal Biosciences, Chai Discovery, and Eikon Therapeutics.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.7x multiplier to the $3.1B last post-money valuation, implying a next-stage value of $5.3B. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies yields a median valuation of $3.8B, with the peer set ranging from $539M (Eikon Therapeutics, Series B) to $10.3B (Colossal Biosciences, Series C). Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales cannot be applied; the sector median P/S is 6.9x across the three public tickers, but New Limit's revenue is not disclosed.

The two applicable methods support a valuation range of $3.2B to $6.1B, with a median cross-section of $4.5B. The wide spread reflects heterogeneity within the biotech peer set—a known challenge in frontier-stage valuation. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory and clinical-stage risk, neither of which the available data addresses.
